results.title: European Data Centres Overview | CBRE Sweden
results.text:
European Data Centres Overview | CBRE Sweden

Intelligent Investment

# European Data Centres Overview

juli 22, 2024 Konsumtionstid

### Looking for a PDF of this content?

Download

### About This Report

Data centres play an increasingly important role in the world. The purpose-built facilities house computer systems and components, such as telecommunications and storage systems, designed to enable business processes, government services, and social interactions.

Data centres form the foundation of the digital economy, hosting the cloud, and storing our data.

However, the role of data centres is not always apparent since data centres are often located in trading estates or industrial sites formerly occupied by heavy industries.

This report aims to provide a better understanding of the fundamentals of the data centre sector. We detail the growth drivers of the asset class, the size of Europe’s largest colocation data centre markets, the type of investors involved, and key considerations of development and investment in the sector.

## Key Takeaways

1GW

Live capacity in London, making it Europe’s largest data centre market

85%

Of take-up was recorded in FLAPD* last year *Represents the five largest colocation markets in Europe: Frankfurt, London, Amsterdam, Paris, and Dublin

5.8GW

Data centre capacity expected across Europe by the end of 2024

Access the full report to gain insights into this major asset class.

## Contacts

### Kevin Restivo

Head of Data Centre Re

# Europe Data Center Market Q3 2023 Insights

In Q3 2023, Europe's data centre market saw a significant increase in both new supply (472MW) and take-up (471MW), indicating a balance between demand and supply. The vacancy rate in primary markets, particularly FLAPD, reached a low of 10.3%, while secondary markets experienced higher vacancy rates. Hyperscalers continue to drive demand, but challenges remain in meeting AI-specific requirements due to limited availability of AI-ready facilities and power constraints.

# CBRE: European hyperscaler self-build capacity growth to outpace colocation supply growth, but supply outpaces take-up in weaker than expected 2025

Hyperscaler self-build to increase by 24 percent in 2026

February 20, 2026 By Barney Dixon Have your say

Growth of European data center capacity operated and owned exclusively by hyperscalers will outpace colocation supply growth in the region this year, according to research from real estate services firm CBRE.

CBRE’s Europe Data Centres report, which covers Q4 2025, predicts hyperscaler self-build capacity will reach 4.2GW in 2026, a 24 percent increase on the previous year and the seventeenth consecutive year of double-digit growth.

In comparison, CBRE expects the top 15 European colocation data center markets to grow 19 percent year-on-year. The colocation segment is expected to remain about 50 percent larger than the self-build segment at the end of 2026.

– Getty Images

This new supply will be delivered across nine European countries, with 60 percent of Europe’s operational hyperscale self-build capacity located in Ireland, the Netherlands, Sweden, and Belgium.

results.title: Global Data Center Trends 2023 | CBRE
results.text:
Global Data Center Trends 2023 | CBRE

Intelligent Investment

# Global Data Center Trends 2023

## New technology is driving record demand but power constraints are inhibiting growth

July 14, 2023 20 Minute Read

- Share on Facebook
- Share on Twitter
- Share on LinkedIn
- Email

## Executive Summary

### Looking for a PDF of this content?

Download

- A worldwide shortage of available power is inhibiting growth of the global data center market. Sourcing enough power is a top priority of data center operators across North America, Europe, Latin America and Asia-Pacific. Certain secondary markets with robust power supplies stand to attract more data center operators.
- New development is occurring across all four regions despite limited power availability. Northern Virginia remains the world's largest data center market with 2,132 megawatts (MW) of total inventory.
- Despite new development, vacancy rates are declining in all four regions due to strong demand. Singapore—the world’s most power-constrained data center market—has less than 4 MW of available capacity and a record-low vacancy rate of less than 2%.
- Large corporations are finding it increasingly difficult to find enough data center capacity. Low supply, construction delays and power challenges are impacting all markets. For example, Querétaro, Mexico, has only 1.2 MW available for lease.
- The worldwide shortage of available supply is leading to price increases for data center capacity. Singapore has the highest

results.title: Colocation Market Tracker | Synergy Research Group
results.text:
Colocation Market Tracker | Synergy Research Group

# Colocation Market Tracker

Published through Q1 2026

The Synergy Research Group Colocation Market Tracker provides quarterly analyses with detailed data on market share, sizing, and forecasts for both retail and wholesale colocation. Breakouts are provided by operator type and provider category, covering service revenues and capacity metrics across the global colocation landscape. Coverage spans Retail Colocation (with Carrier Neutral and Bandwidth Provider detail) and Wholesale Colocation (with Hyperscale and Enterprise/Service Provider detail).

Data is published quarterly for worldwide, regional, and major country markets, with full access through published reports and Synergy’s proprietary online platform, SIA. The service tracks more than 270 colocation providers, with historical metrics extending back over ten years.

In addition, the tracker covers 91 individual metro markets, including more than 30 U.S. metros, 4 Canadian metros, 5 Latin American metros, over 30 EMEA metros, and 17 APAC metros, with coverage continuing to expand.

results.title: The “FLAP” Markets Bring in 55% of W. European Colocation Revenues; Equinix and Digital Realty are the Leaders | Synergy Research Group
results.text:
The “FLAP” Markets Bring in 55% of W. European Colocation Revenues; Equinix and Digital Realty are the Leaders | Synergy Research Group

## The “FLAP” Markets Bring in 55% of W. European Colocation Revenues; Equinix and Digital Realty are the Leaders

RENO, NV, October 18, 2022

New data from Synergy Research Group shows that the metros of Frankfurt, London, Amsterdam and Paris (the so-called FLAP markets) together account for 55% of the Western European colocation market, based on retail and wholesale colocation revenues in Q2 2022. London remains by far the largest metro market, followed by Frankfurt and then, at a distance, Paris and Amsterdam. The next six metro markets are relatively much smaller and combined are no bigger than Frankfurt. Outside the top ten metros, the entire rest of region accounts for less than a third of the market. It is notable that Equinix is a very clear market leader in all four FLAP markets, with Digital Realty ranked second in three of them. Consequently those two are also the market leaders for the whole region. The next largest colocation providers in Western Europe are NTT, Telehouse, Global Switch and Deutsche Telekom. Other notable players in the FLAP markets include Data4, NorthC, STACK infrastructure, Virtus, CyrusOne, Iron Mountain, Orange and EdgeConneX. By segment Equinix, Digital Realty and Telehouse are the leaders in retail colocation while the wholesale market is led by Global Switch, NTT and Digital Realty. Over the last two yea

# Hyperscale Market Tracker

Published through Q1 2026

The Synergy Research Group Hyperscale Cloud Market Tracker monitors the small group of companies that operate global networks of massive data centers, supporting a rapidly growing share of the cloud services market. These hyperscale operators account for an expanding portion of industry spend on computing, storage, and networking technologies, with economies of scale serving as a key driver of cloud growth. Generative AI has further elevated the role of hyperscale operators, positioning them as critical players at the forefront of IT industry developments.

results.title: On-Premise Data Center Capacity Being Increasingly ...
results.text:
On-Premise Data Center Capacity Being Increasingly Dwarfed by Hyperscalers and Colocation Companies | Synergy Research Group

## On-Premise Data Center Capacity Being Increasingly Dwarfed by Hyperscalers and Colocation Companies

RENO, NV, July 12, 2023

As the number of large data centers operated by hyperscale providers approaches 900, new data from Synergy Research Group shows that they now account for 37% of the worldwide capacity of all data centers. Approximately half of that hyperscale capacity is in own-built, owned data centers and half is in leased facilities. With non-hyperscale colocation capacity accounting for another 23% of capacity, that leaves on-premise data centers with just 40% of the total. This is in stark contrast to five years ago, when almost 60% of data center capacity was in on-premise facilities. Looking ahead five years, hyperscale operators will account for over half of all capacity, while on-premise will drop to under 30%. Meanwhile the total capacity of all data centers will continue to rise steadily, driven primarily by hyperscale capacity almost doubling over the next five years. While on-premise share of the total will drop by over two percentage points per year, the actual capacity of on-premise data centers will decrease only marginally. Colocation share of total capacity will remain relatively constant. The hyperscale research is based on an analysis of the data center footprint and operations of 19 of the world’s major cloud and internet

Category: European Data Centre Reports Tags: Colocation, Data Centre Supply Report, Europe, Frankfurt

- Description

## Description

Frankfurt, Germany continues to be one of the largest data centre markets in Europe and the world. The current built-out capacity of the market was nearly 650MW in 2022 and is projected to climb to over 900MW by the end of 2024. This is a market that will be worth just over €2b in 2024 and is projected to grow at a 5-year CAGR of 19.4%. Hyperscale colocation is growing at a five-year CAGR of 26.1% and retail colocation is moving along at a 10.3% clip.

The underlying fundamentals of the Frankfurt market remain strong. Hyperscale clouds have built out significant footprints and the AZ architecture has solidified. Cloud adoption remains in its early stages, with uptake hindered by stringent data residency regulations and a generally conservative IT culture. But some of that is starting to loosen, with the pandemic shifting attitudes towards outsourcing, and data residency laws continuing to push demand for infras

## the EUDCA

It is with great pride as president of the European Data Centre Association (EUDCA), I can present the second edition of our State of European Data Centres, for 2026.

Building on last year’s milestone achievement, it is only possible through the invaluable contribution and cooperation of our members, and we are excited to share the results of this comprehensive research with the public. This year has seen the deepening impact of artificial intelligence, the continued emergence of new centres of activity, and the constant issue of energy availability. Building on the expanding resource that is the Energy Efficiency Directive (EED) data, the report reflects an industry that is experiencing rapid growth even amid the challenges of a more demanding regulatory environment and changing geopolitical and cybersecurity landscapes. results.text:
State of European Data Centres 2025 | eudca.org

# Inaugural Flagship Report - State of European Data Centres 2025 Click here to download​​

## State of European Data Centres 2025

The State of European Data Centres 2025 is the inaugural flagship report from the European Data Centre Association (EUDCA), offering an unprecedented deep dive into the infrastructure that powers Europe’s digital economy. Developed in collaboration with National Trade Associations and leading researchers, this full report goes far beyond the management summary - presenting a large, data-driven examination of the economic, environmental, and technological landscape of Europe’s colocation and hyperscale data centres.

Inside, the report reveals how €100 billion in investments are reshaping Europe’s data centre landscape, advancing AI, boosting digital sovereignty, and expanding from FLAP-D hubs to fast-growing Nordic and Southern regions. It highlights a projected €83.8 billion GDP impact and over 80,000 jobs by 2030, while addressing key challenges like power access, skills shortages, and sustainability, underscored by the sector’s use of 94% renewable energy.

Whether you’re a policymaker, investor, data centre operator, or digital strategist, this report is the critical intelligence you need to navigate the future of digital infrastructure in Europe.

results.title: New 2026 State of European Data Centres | eudca.org
results.text:
New 2026 State of European Data Centres | eudca.org

Skip to Content

## New State of European Data Centres 2026 report

## State of European Data Centres 2026

The State of European Data Centres 2026 is EUDCA’s flagship annual report, delivering a data-driven overview of Europe’s data centre sector. Developed with national trade associations (NTAs) and leading researchers, the report examines the infrastructure supporting Europe’s digital economy and the forces that will shape its future.

The 2026 edition shows that commercial colocation and hyperscale facilities now provide more than two-thirds of Europe’s IT power, driven by the growing demand for cloud and AI. It explores shifting regional growth patterns across Southern Europe and the Nordics, alongside the impact of new connectivity, renewable energy investment and rising power constraints.

With European IT power demand forecast to grow at a CAGR of 17% through 2031, the report analyses the key challenges, including access to power, cooling innovation, and its growing socio-economic and sustainability contribution. Colocation data centres significantly advanced Europe's 2030 energy, water, and climate goals in 2025 alone, contributing €53 billion to the continent's GDP.

Whether you’re a policymaker, investor, data centre operator, or digital strategist, this report is the critical intelligence you need to navigate the future of digital infrastructure in Europe.
